stem: A agricultural research study surveys 20 seeds planted and observes 10 positive outcomes out of 20 (50.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (10 observations, rate = 40.0%) and Group B (10 observations, rate = 60.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 33% and 52% of the population. Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.5. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.5011 (range [0.4804, 0.5241]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2993, 0.7007]. Point estimate: 0.5011.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.43: 0.43×0.37 + 0.57×0.6 = 0.5011.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
